input.prompt:
I have two ASP.NET Core 8 services communicating via RabbitMQ. Service A publishes
an order event and Service B consumes it. Traces show up fine within each service
but I can't see the end-to-end distributed trace across the queue boundary.
How do I propagate the trace context through message headers? Don't create files.

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about propagating OpenTelemetry/distributed trace context across RabbitMQ message boundaries in ASP.NET Core 8. They want to see end-to-end traces across Service A (publisher) and Service B (consumer). They don't want me to create files — just explain the approach.

This is a knowledge question, not a code task. Let me give a concise, actionable answer.

The key concept: W3C Trace Context propagation via RabbitMQ message headers. You need to manually inject the trace context into message headers on the publisher side and extract it on the consumer side, then start a new activity linked to that context.


I'll walk through the pattern: when publishing, grab the current activity's trace ID and parent span ID, serialize them into the message headers using the W3C standard format, then on the consumer side extract those headers and use them to create a linked activity that continues the trace chain.
